info.parenttitle: Japanese expansion in East Asia - Causes of expansion

markdown:
## **Political instability in China**


![Qing Dynasty ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/6eef889cf6a324638e70eaccb3c4f1e83d3ac76b-2000x1498.jpg)

### **1. Chinese Empire**


1. **The Chinese Empire had been ruled by dynasties for centuries.**
  1. But** during the 19th C they suffered a series of military defeats **resulting in **foreign countries exploiting it. **
  2. This in turn** undermined the dynastic rule **until **in 1911 the last dynasty, the Qing/Manchu was dethroned and China became a Republic.**

<callout type="note">* There are** two systems to transliterate** **Chinese characters into English: Pinyin **and** Wade-Giles. **
* That is why in **different books you can find the same concept or name written in such a different way.** 
* For example**, Qing and Manchu are two transliterations of the same name of the dynasty**. 
* **Both are accepted and both versions will appear in your Paper 1,** so don’t worry!</callout>

****

![Qing Dynasty](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/cc2deee9650b17973b5de3b267bb0eddf5023f97-1600x1553.jpg)

### **2. Guomindang (GMD)/Kuomintang (KMT)**


1. **The Guomindang (GMD)/Kuomintang (KMT)** was the **party that took down the Qing**
  1. But they were **unable to unite China** and **the territory got split into regional political units** led by strong and personalistic individuals. 
  2. This was the** Warlord Era** (1916-1927).
2. **In 1926-1927 the Northern Expedition, aimed at defeating the warlords and unifying China.**
3. **The expedition was led by Chiang Kai-shek (Jiang Jieshi)** and, by the end of 1927, he had established a **new Nationalist government based in Nanjing.**

<callout type="note">* The **unification of China is a very complex process**, and it involves the key input of the** Chinese Communist Party **led by Mao and **oscillating relations with the USSR. **
* These topics will be discussed in depth for **Paper 2 topic 10: Authoritarian States.**
* For **Paper 1, we will focus on the role this political instability had for the Japanese-Chinese relations.**</callout>

****

![Chiang Kai-shek](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/1b8394b9d4441ae893c8187870f41348a517bc7e-1110x1501.png)

### **3. Manchuria **


1. In **Manchuria **the situation was **different, and very volatile.** 
2. The **warlord leading Manchuria was Zhang Zoulin,** and he had declared independence in 1922. 
  1. **The initial attitude of the Japanese had been one of uneasy collaboration. **
  2. **In exchange, Zhang had allowed Japan to continue developing railways, mills, mines and other businesses.**
3. Nevertheless, as militarism and authoritarianism grew in Japan, some senior Kwantung Army officers** viewed Zhang's autonomy as detrimental to Japan.**
  1. They thought that **a weaker Manchuria would be better for Japan´s interests**. 
  2. In 1928, the **Kwantung army killed Zhang Zoulin** but **this backfired for Japan: his son took over and allied with the GMD.**

<callout type="note">The** Kwantung Army** was Japan's** elite military force in Manchuria, and they were very influential in Japanese expansionist policy.**</callout>

****

![Zhang Zoulin](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a38e0f9e1f160b2c3c0e562491a3b8dbe839236a-1748x2331.jpg)

### **4. Chinese Unification**


1. **This allowed the Northern Expedition to culminate with the unification of China under the GMD and Chiang Kai Shek´s leadership**. 
  1. **Japan's concerns grew,** and they **sent more Kwantung army soldiers to be stationed in the Liaodong Peninsula,** to protect Japan's installations and investments.
2. **By that time, the Warlord Era had come to an end, and with it Japanese foreign policy towards China changed dramatically. **
  1. For the** Japanese, the newly unified China appeared as a threat to their interests**
  2. Especially because the** GMD came to power with a proposal of ending all foreign concessions in China.**

<callout type="tip">* While you're preparing this topic**, look at a map of the Manchurian region and the reasons why Japan would be keen on controlling the area**
* **Look at other territories that Japan had authority on, for example Korea.**</callout>

![Warlord Era](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/0629131cfa55d1a31534440005cd67f290732c62-1235x955.png)

<callout type="note">**Japan's aggression from 1931 onwards can be seen as a response to this changing political situation in China**, alongside the **economic and political needs Japan had derived from the impact of the Great Depression.**</callout>
